recent_calls.summary: The user, Jason Ray, reported an issue with his water heater that won't stay on but does not involve a gas leak. The agent documented the issue as urgent and informed Jason that the team will contact him on the next business day since it was after hours. The call ended with the user declining further assistance.
The user called with a problem related to Houston and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent confirmed hearing the user and proceeded with the transfer promptly.

The user, Aika from American Home Shield, requested to speak to a live representative and the agent transferred the call successfully to another agent. The call lasted 18 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user, Aika from American Home Shield escalation team, called to discuss a dispatch and requested that an updated report be submitted to the authorization team regarding a replacement option for the homeowner. The agent acknowledged the request and then transferred the call to a live representative as requested.

The user called to inquire about a service they had already booked through American Home Shield and requested a callback. The agent collected the user's contact details and service address, then transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to the billing department.

The user called to confirm if Budget was open on Memorial Day and mentioned they already had a scheduled service for that day. The agent confirmed the hours and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred without issues.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

Christopher Ross called to check the status of his appointment and inquire about the technician or plumber assigned. The agent confirmed the appointment time and noted Christopher's request for plumber R. Love, then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live representative.

De Juan Lockhart called to get an update on a home warranty service request for his central air unit, which is completely out and causing discomfort due to heat. The agent confirmed the details, noted the urgency for service as soon as possible, and informed De Juan that the team would reach out shortly. De Juan declined additional maintenance program information and provided positive feedback on the call experience.

Ashley called to report that her HVAC system is still leaking in the basement after a float valve was installed a couple of months ago. The agent confirmed the details and assured her that the team would be notified to send someone for a diagnostic visit. The call ended with the agent confirming contact information and service address.
The call was very brief and the agent attempted to engage the user, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.

The user, Leo, called to schedule an urgent plumbing appointment for an emergency involving a completely clogged toilet. The agent documented the emergency, updated the user's contact information and service address, and prioritized the request for the next business day since it was after hours. The call ended with the user confirming no further assistance was needed.

The user, James Ell Kee, reported that his air conditioner was not working and requested service as soon as possible the next day due to his wife being in a wheelchair and needing extra comfort. The agent collected all necessary details, confirmed the service request, and informed James that the team would follow up the next business day. James expressed satisfaction with the agent's understanding and assistance.

The user called to confirm that Robert Ryan Love would be their plumber for an appointment at 10 AM the next day. The agent noted the request but could not confirm technician assignments after hours and informed the user that the team would follow up after 8 AM the next day. The user was advised to call back after 8 AM for confirmation.
The user spoke incoherently and did not engage in a clear conversation with the agent. The call ended abruptly without any resolution or service request.
The user called Budget's after-hours assistant but quickly realized they had the wrong number and ended the call shortly after.

Cristen called to request emergency plumbing service for no hot water at Panda Express in Columbia. The agent documented and escalated the request but informed Cristen that no live representative or technician could be dispatched after hours. Cristen then repeatedly asked to cancel the request, expressing frustration about the lack of immediate service.

The user, Demarcus, returned a call from the previous day and wanted to know the reason for the call. The agent informed him that they do not have access to that information but assured that the team will follow up on the next business day. No further assistance was requested.

Kanisha called to set up an emergency appointment for a water heater issue causing no hot water. The agent confirmed the details, updated the service address, and escalated the issue for priority follow-up since it was outside regular business hours. The call ended with the agent assuring Kanisha that the team will contact her on the next business day.

The user, Mark Parres, reported a gas leak in his house and requested a contractor to check the leak. The agent acknowledged the urgency, documented the issue, and escalated the request for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further assistance needed at the moment.

The user, Alfonso Cozzi, called to report a heating issue where the thermostat is locked at 82 degrees and cannot be unlocked. The agent gathered all necessary information including contact details, service address, and availability, and confirmed the issue is a comfort concern, not an emergency. The user was informed that the team will follow up on the next business day to schedule service.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly without any conversation beyond the initial greeting from the agent.

The user reported an emergency with their hot water heater not working. The agent documented the issue, escalated it for next business day follow-up, and informed the user that immediate assistance was not available after hours. The user declined further help and the call ended politely.

The user, Flint, called to confirm an appointment for air conditioning service scheduled for Monday at 2 PM. The issue involves the AC unit not working with a frozen line, likely due to low Freon, and the unit uses older style Freon. The agent noted all details and informed Flint that the team will confirm the appointment on the next business day.
The user requested to speak to a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Yui from American Home Shield, called to confirm the status of a report or diagnosis. The agent was unable to provide the information directly and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect them with a live representative. The call was quickly transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user called Budget customer service, provided their ZIP code 63122, and requested to speak with a live agent. The call was then transferred successfully to another agent for further assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user inquired whether their estimate or work order was sent to American Home Shield. The agent informed the user that they do not have real-time access but will forward the request to the team for follow-up. The call ended with the user declining further assistance.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to engage in conversation, but the user did not respond and ended the call shortly after.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Flint Couse, called to schedule an appointment for AC unit service due to a suspected Freon issue. The agent successfully booked a provisional appointment for Monday at 2 PM and collected feedback from the user, who rated the experience positively. The call concluded with the user satisfied and no further issues.

Donna called to set up an appointment for air conditioner service under her American Home Shield home warranty. She scheduled a morning appointment for Tuesday after the weekend and will provide the dispatch number later. The agent confirmed the details and offered information about maintenance programs, which Donna declined, and she rated the service experience positively.

Demarcus called to schedule a heating and cooling service appointment through American Home Shield for the afternoon of June 11th. After some delay in locating the dispatch number, he provided it, and the agent confirmed the details before ending the call. Demarcus declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the assistance received.

The user, Christopher Ross, scheduled a plumbing service appointment for a cartridge replacement at his home in Ballwin, Missouri. The agent confirmed the earliest available time on May 25th at 10 AM and arranged to email the estimate invoice to the user. The call concluded with the user declining to participate in a feedback survey.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.

The call involved a discussion about a dispatch for Harold and Marillie Salzer, where the user requested to proceed with the replacement option for the whole unit instead of just the compressor repair. The agent confirmed the request would be passed along and mentioned potential delays due to the holiday. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to speak with a live agent and was promptly transferred to one by the initial agent. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed the transfer successfully.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed the transfer successfully.

The user called and was greeted by the agent Sophie, who attempted to assist but quickly transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.

Alex from American Home Shield called on behalf of a member regarding an ongoing service issue where the homeowner cannot turn on the unit because the technician removed all the Freon during the last visit. The agent acknowledged the issue, confirmed the service address and contact details, and assured that the team would follow up promptly to arrange a technician visit, preferably the same day. The call ended with the agent confirming no further assistance was needed.
